summary:
The short sharp pain associated with a toothache is primarily conveyed via Aδ fibers due to their rapid conduction properties for acute painful stimuli compared to slower conducting C-fibers. Key here is understanding that these specific nerve pathways efficiently communicate intense but brief sensory information directly related to oral discomfort reaching higher cortical regions including parts responsible for processing such inputs as 'brain' implies involvement at central nervous system levels beyond just peripheral transmission mechanisms alone. 

Note I kept it concise while focusing only on clinically significant aspects regarding how this type of pain signal travels between affected areas within mouth structures up into cerebral cortex where
